title: 360°_Bloom content-time hold across A→B hard cut (BLOCKER-B)
goal: 360°_Bloom peak collapse (f13-17 ~6-8 dB) is NOT colour space (T-qbloomlin01 REFUTED linear-chain: linear bloom 9.04 < sRGB-float 10.48 < OFF 11.51). ROOT: the A/B split is a HARD CUT at t=0.1333s (Transition A timing out=20480/153600=0.1333s; B in=0.1333 out=0.25, no crossfade). The 3 filters (Gaussian Blur/Glow/Bloom) sit on the GROUP wrapping A+B. GUI-GT peak f13-14=(255,227,199) is warm-WHITE = bloomed A HELD across the cut (warm tint = A, not cool B). Engine switches content to B at f13 so it blooms B (cool/grey). FIX: hold the pre-cut bloomed content (bloomed A) across the peak while the Glow/Bloom filter TIME plays through its 0.1167->0.15s max (Radius 90, Threshold 3/0.1) — a CONTENT-time clamp decoupled from FILTER-time, the same BLOCKER-B coupling documented for Lights__Bloom. Must be generic (fires on >=2 built-ins: Lights__Bloom + 360°_Bloom) and gate all 65. Only AFTER the content-hold lands is the threshold-onset curve decode (still undecoded; flat-tangent bezier + smoothstep both WORSE) worth revisiting.
